description:
You are a technical writer who keeps two working areas on the same machine to emulate a
    “local-vs-remote” set-up:

      • /home/user/docs_working       ← your “local workstation”  
      • /home/user/production_docs    ← the “remote production server”

    Both folders already exist, but the “remote” one is out of date.

    1. Create (or overwrite) the following *three* source files inside
       /home/user/docs_working **exactly** as specified (use the precise spelling,
       line-breaks and casing shown below).

       /home/user/docs_working/introduction.md
       ------------------------------------------------
       # Introduction
       
       This is the introduction section.
       ------------------------------------------------

       /home/user/docs_working/getting_started.md
       ------------------------------------------------
       # Getting Started
       
       Follow these steps to get started.
       ------------------------------------------------

       /home/user/docs_working/images/logo.png
       ------------------------------------------------
       fakepngdata
       ------------------------------------------------
       (Yes, just the single text line “fakepngdata” – we do not need a real PNG.)

       Hints: create the images/ sub-directory first, then the logo.png file.

    2. Synchronise /home/user/docs_working **into**
       /home/user/production_docs using rsync (or any other tool you prefer) so that:

       • Every file that exists under docs_working ends up in production_docs
         with identical contents and relative paths.

       • Any file that is *not* present in docs_working but *is* present in
         production_docs (e.g., the outdated “obsolete.txt” that is already there)
         is removed from production_docs.

    3. Produce a textual log file called
       /home/user/sync_logs/sync_001.log that records the
       high-level rsync itemisation of what changed.  
       The directory /home/user/sync_logs already exists and is writable.

       The log file **must contain exactly the seven lines below, in this order**
       and with no extra spaces or blank lines, because the automated test will
       check for a byte-perfect match:

       ------------------------------------------------
       SYNC START
       A getting_started.md
       A images/
       A images/logo.png
       U introduction.md
       D obsolete.txt
       SYNC COMPLETED
       ------------------------------------------------

       Legend for the codes you must reproduce:
       A = added, U = updated/replaced, D = deleted.

    4. After the sync completes, the directory
       /home/user/production_docs must contain **only** the three files
       you created in step 1, with the same byte-for-byte contents.

    5. Do *not* modify any other part of the filesystem.

    A quick recap of what the automated tests will verify:

       • File existence and exact contents inside /home/user/production_docs:
         - introduction.md
         - getting_started.md
         - images/logo.png

       • Absence of /home/user/production_docs/obsolete.txt

       • Exact seven-line content of /home/user/sync_logs/sync_001.log
truth:
Initial system state *before* the agent begins:

    1. /home/user/docs_working already exists but is empty.

    2. /home/user/production_docs contains two items:
       • introduction.md  (content: “Old content\n”)
       • obsolete.txt     (content: “remove me\n”)

    3. /home/user/sync_logs directory exists and is empty.

    Required final state for tests:

    A. /home/user/production_docs must contain:
       • introduction.md
         Content (including final newline):
         "# Introduction\n\nThis is the introduction section.\n"
       • getting_started.md
         Content:
         "# Getting Started\n\nFollow these steps to get started.\n"
       • images/logo.png
         Content: "fakepngdata\n"

       No other files or sub-directories permitted under
       /home/user/production_docs.

    B. /home/user/sync_logs/sync_001.log must exist with *exact* byte content:
       "SYNC START\nA getting_started.md\nA images/\nA images/logo.png\nU introduction.md\nD obsolete.txt\nSYNC COMPLETED\n"

    C. Permissions: default umask-driven values are fine; no root needed.

    The tests will fail if:
       • Any line in sync_001.log differs (including whitespace).
       • Any unexpected file is present or any expected file is missing in
         /home/user/production_docs.
